interest: Equity advocates and low-SES/Roma families whose gains from formal reform could be quietly erased by informal selection channels, alongside teachers who refuse to absorb opaque discretionary selection burdens.
value: Equity and procedural transparency versus institutional autonomy and unregulated professional discretion.
fear: That formal reform becomes a facade — coaching-resistant exams are replaced by interviews and reputation-based filtering that re-advantage high-SES families and expose teachers to unchecked, unaccountable discretion.
affected: high-SES families (as beneficiaries of informal channels)
assumption: That schools facing a reformed formal channel will actively substitute informal selection mechanisms unless explicitly monitored and constrained, rather than complying with the reform's intent.
empirical_uncertainty: Contested/unknown: whether informal selection proliferates post-reform is untested in the Hungarian context and is precisely what the proposed pilot phase and independent monitoring are meant to resolve.
